commit_scope: Report remaining Python-dependent bootstrap surfaces as Form-native transmutation candidates in wellness

local_validation.summary: The Bootstrap compost wellness section now reports named Python transmutation candidates and automatically classifies path-named substrate Python files that are not yet listed in the compost manifest from repeated lexical/path edge attributes into metabolic categories with scores, signals, file names, release gates, and admission/release protocol. Static surfaces now report wants_dynamic rows with edge_reputation counts to dynamic successors, and the manifest is documented as an attested release ledger rather than the complete candidate universe.

ci_validation.reason: No commit or PR created in this turn.

deploy_validation.reason: Local reporting and documentation change only; no runtime deployment was performed.

phase_gate.reason: Local validation passed, but no CI or deployment validation ran in this turn.

evidence_refs: make wellness output includes `Python transmutation candidates — 13 named Python-dependent files / 9826 LOC still asking for Form-native release gates`.

make wellness output includes adapter parser/emitter, adapter CLI/scripts, bridge/runtime, utility endpoint rows, and corrected substrate perimeter math: 28 path-named, manifest-unlisted files / 14623 LOC after subtracting only the 4 Phase C files that live in api/app/services/substrate/.

make wellness output includes automatic metabolic categories for the manifest-unlisted perimeter: source grammar/parser residue, recipe realization/runtime semantics, domain vocabulary/cell authoring, format/source frontend, substrate identity/persistence infrastructure, and observation/proof metabolism.
make wellness output includes dynamic admission, manifest admission, and release criteria for path-named, manifest-unlisted cells.
make wellness output includes `Static-to-dynamic wants` rows for static ledgers/snapshots, and those rows feed `what_wants_attention` through the `wants_dynamic:` marker.
make wellness output includes `edge_reputation` rows that compress repeated static edge events into a reputation count between a static source cell and dynamic successor cell.
kernels/BOOTSTRAP_COMPOST_MANIFEST.md now states that dynamic candidate admission happens in wellness and static manifest rows are attested release-ledger entries.
kernels/BOOTSTRAP_COMPOST_MANIFEST.md now states that repeated static edges release into dynamic edge categories with reputation counts instead of storing every event.
